entries.canonical_fix_summary: Prevent stored XSS by treating persisted user-controlled content as untrusted at render time, applying context-appropriate encoding or sanitization, and avoiding unsafe HTML rendering APIs unless content is explicitly sanitized for that sink.

framework_variants.summary: Encode stored content when rendering templates or API-driven HTML and sanitize any user-authored rich text before it reaches an HTML sink.
recommended_actions: Use templating features that escape by default and review any raw HTML rendering helpers.
Apply a reviewed HTML sanitization step for stored rich text before rendering it to users.

framework_variants.summary: Treat persisted content as untrusted whenever it is returned to templates or frontend consumers that may render HTML, and sanitize rich text for the actual sink.
recommended_actions: Keep templates and response generation on escaping-by-default paths.
Review stored-content rendering endpoints and admin previews for raw HTML injection.

framework_variants.summary: Rely on Django's autoescaping and reserve `safe`-style rendering for content that has been intentionally sanitized for the target context.
recommended_actions: Remove unsafe template filters or flags that bypass escaping for stored content.
Sanitize user-authored HTML before rendering and review admin previews and email templates.

framework_variants.summary: Keep server-side rendering on escaping-by-default paths and sanitize persisted rich text before passing it to HTML-capable views or downstream clients.
recommended_actions: Avoid raw HTML insertion helpers for stored content unless a trusted sanitization step has already occurred.
Review MVC views, email generation, and content-preview endpoints for unsafe rendering.

framework_variants.summary: Use autoencoding Razor or equivalent safe rendering paths and sanitize stored rich text before exposing it to HTML-capable sinks.
recommended_actions: Remove raw HTML helpers for untrusted stored content unless it is explicitly sanitized for the sink.
Review admin UIs, previews, and email templates for persisted content rendered without encoding.

framework_variants.summary: Render stored content as text by default and avoid `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` or equivalent unsafe sink usage unless content has gone through a reviewed HTML sanitization pipeline.
recommended_actions: Keep persisted content on text-rendering paths where possible.
Sanitize rich text on the server or trusted processing layer before any HTML injection point is used.

validation_steps: Replay stored XSS payloads through creation and viewing flows and confirm they render harmlessly to all viewers.
Add tests proving stored user content is encoded or sanitized before it reaches HTML-capable sinks.
Review preview, export, email, and admin-rendering paths for the same unsafe content pattern.
unsafe_alternatives: Blocking a few tags or strings while still allowing attacker-controlled HTML to reach the sink.
Sanitizing on input only and assuming every later rendering context is safe.
Using client-side filtering as the main protection while server-rendered or alternate viewers stay vulnerable.

references.label: OWASP Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
CWE-79 Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

sources.label: OWASP Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
CWE-79 Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

entries.title: Reduce verbose error disclosure while preserving diagnosability
entries.canonical_fix_summary: Return generic client-facing errors for untrusted callers, keep stack traces and internal diagnostics out of responses, and route detailed failure data to protected logs or telemetry instead.

framework_variants.summary: Use centralized error middleware to return stable, non-sensitive responses while keeping stack traces and internals in protected server logs only.
recommended_actions: Replace raw exception serialization with generic client error bodies.
Review development flags and debug middleware so production does not expose stack traces or config details.

framework_variants.summary: Configure exception handlers to return minimal client-facing messages and keep traceback, dependency internals, and validation specifics out of production responses.
recommended_actions: Use generic error schemas for untrusted clients and send details to protected logs.
Review debug settings and framework defaults that may leak tracebacks or internal model details.

framework_variants.summary: Disable production debug behavior and keep template, stack, and settings details out of user-visible error pages and API responses.
recommended_actions: Ensure production uses safe error handlers and generic server-error responses.
Review custom exception pages, DRF error handlers, and admin tools for leaked internals.

framework_variants.summary: Return controlled error payloads from exception handling and prevent stack traces, bean details, or configuration metadata from reaching untrusted callers.
recommended_actions: Use centralized exception mapping with minimal response content.
Review actuator, error pages, and debug settings for accidental internal disclosure.

framework_variants.summary: Use production-safe exception handling so clients receive generic errors while detailed stack and environment information stays in protected telemetry.
recommended_actions: Configure middleware and exception filters to avoid returning raw exception details.
Review development exception pages, API problem details, and logging enrichers for oversharing.

framework_variants.summary: Keep server and route errors generic for users and avoid exposing stack traces, env details, or backend messages in rendered pages and API responses.
recommended_actions: Use controlled error boundaries and API route handlers that avoid leaking internals.
Review server-rendering failures, route handlers, and client error overlays so production responses stay minimal.

validation_steps: Trigger representative failures and confirm responses do not reveal stack traces, internal paths, config values, or sensitive implementation details.
Verify detailed diagnostics are still available in protected logs or telemetry for operators.
Review production configuration to ensure debug or developer exception modes are disabled.
unsafe_alternatives: Hiding only the message text while still returning stack traces or internal object dumps.
Conditionally exposing detailed errors based on easily spoofed request headers or query parameters.
Relying on client-side handling alone while backend APIs continue to leak internal exceptions.

references.label: OWASP Error Handling Cheat Sheet
CWE-209 Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

sources.label: OWASP Error Handling Cheat Sheet
CWE-209 Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

entries.title: Prevent prototype pollution by constraining object merge and property paths
entries.canonical_fix_summary: Stop untrusted input from modifying prototype chains or special object properties by validating merge keys, using safe object creation patterns, and avoiding dangerous recursive merge behavior on attacker-controlled data.

framework_variants.summary: Validate object keys before merging request data and avoid unsafe deep-merge helpers that can write to `__proto__`, `constructor`, or `prototype` paths.
recommended_actions: Use allow-listed properties and safe object creation when shaping request data.
Review body parsing, config merge helpers, and utility libraries for attacker-controlled recursive merges.

framework_variants.summary: Keep request data on validated schema paths and avoid dynamic object updates or merge helpers that let untrusted keys reach internal object structures.
recommended_actions: Validate incoming fields against explicit models and reject unexpected property names.
Review helper functions that recursively merge dict-like input into application objects.

framework_variants.summary: Constrain request-derived object updates and avoid dynamic merge helpers or deserialization paths that let attacker-chosen keys alter internal object behavior.
recommended_actions: Use explicit field allow-lists for object construction and updates.
Review custom JSON merge utilities, form cleaning shortcuts, and admin helpers for dangerous property handling.

framework_variants.summary: Restrict dynamic property binding and avoid mapping or merge utilities that let untrusted input alter object prototypes or special internal fields.
recommended_actions: Allow-list bindable properties and reject unexpected nested keys.
Review JSON mapping, patch endpoints, and utility merges for unsafe recursive behavior.

framework_variants.summary: Limit request-driven property binding and avoid unsafe merge logic that lets attacker-controlled property paths mutate unexpected object state.
recommended_actions: Use explicit DTOs and allow-listed property updates instead of generic object merging.
Review patch handlers, JSON merge helpers, and reflection-based utilities for dangerous key handling.

framework_variants.summary: Validate and constrain client-side and server-side object merges fed by untrusted JSON, especially in state helpers and API route processing.
recommended_actions: Reject dangerous property names and avoid deep-merging arbitrary user objects into shared state or config.
Review route handlers, state hydration, and utility libraries for prototype-write behavior.

validation_steps: Replay payloads containing `__proto__`, `constructor`, and similar special keys and confirm application behavior is unaffected.
Add tests proving unexpected keys are rejected or ignored before merge or binding.
Review recursive merge and dynamic update helpers across the codebase for the same unsafe pattern.
unsafe_alternatives: Blocking only one special key while leaving equivalent prototype-write paths available.
Depending solely on library version upgrades without validating how untrusted objects are merged in local code.
Sanitizing top-level keys while nested attacker-controlled objects still reach dangerous merge logic.

references.label: Prototype Pollution Prevention Cheat Sheet
CWE-1321 Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes ('Prototype Pollution')

sources.label: Prototype Pollution Prevention Cheat Sheet
CWE-1321 Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes ('Prototype Pollution')

entries.title: Eliminate catastrophic backtracking in attacker-influenced regular expressions
entries.canonical_fix_summary: Replace vulnerable regular expressions and usage patterns that allow catastrophic backtracking with bounded, safe patterns or non-regex parsing approaches, especially when the input is attacker-controlled.

framework_variants.summary: Review regexes used in request validation, routing helpers, and content parsing, and replace vulnerable patterns with bounded alternatives.
recommended_actions: Remove nested repetition and ambiguous backtracking from attacker-facing regexes.
Apply input length limits and safe parsing strategies where regex complexity remains hard to bound.

framework_variants.summary: Use safe validation patterns for attacker-controlled input and avoid overly complex regexes in request handling, filtering, and parsing helpers.
recommended_actions: Simplify vulnerable expressions or replace them with parser logic that has predictable runtime.
Bound input sizes for expensive parsing paths that still use regexes.

framework_variants.summary: Audit regexes in URL routing, form validation, and text parsing to ensure attacker-controlled input cannot trigger catastrophic matching behavior.
recommended_actions: Simplify ambiguous expressions and prefer explicit parsing for complex formats.
Apply reasonable size limits to request fields that still require regex validation.

framework_variants.summary: Avoid risky regular expressions in request processing, validation, and content filtering when attackers can influence the input size or structure.
recommended_actions: Refactor vulnerable patterns into safer equivalents or dedicated parsers.
Bound input length on regex-backed validation and parsing paths.

framework_variants.summary: Use safer patterns or explicit parsers for attacker-influenced input and configure regex execution safeguards where supported.
recommended_actions: Replace catastrophic expressions and avoid user-controlled complex matching paths.
Use timeouts and input limits as defense in depth, not the only remediation.

framework_variants.summary: Keep expensive regex validation out of attacker-controlled client and server paths, and replace risky patterns with bounded validation or parsing logic.
recommended_actions: Simplify vulnerable regexes in route handlers, validators, and UI parsing helpers.
Bound input size and avoid trusting client-side validation as the sole protection.

validation_steps: Benchmark or fuzz known worst-case inputs and confirm the regex path no longer exhibits pathological runtime.
Add tests covering maliciously long or adversarial input strings for the affected pattern.
Review similar regexes in validators, routers, and parsers for the same backtracking structure.
unsafe_alternatives: Keeping the vulnerable regex and relying only on request rate limiting or WAF rules.
Adding input size limits without fixing a catastrophic pattern that still triggers at realistic sizes.
Switching to another complex regex with similar ambiguous repetition behavior.

references.label: OWASP Input Validation Cheat Sheet
CWE-1333 Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity

sources.label: OWASP Input Validation Cheat Sheet
CWE-1333 Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity

entries.title: Prevent template injection by keeping untrusted input out of template code paths
entries.canonical_fix_summary: Do not treat untrusted input as template code, template names, or expression fragments; keep template structure server-controlled, pass untrusted data only as plain data, and use sandboxed or safe rendering options where relevant.

framework_variants.summary: Keep template names, template source, and expression construction server-controlled in view rendering paths, and pass user content only as data to escaping-by-default templates.
recommended_actions: Remove any dynamic template compilation or raw helper evaluation fed by request input.
Review view engines, email templates, and admin preview features for untrusted template fragments.

framework_variants.summary: Do not let request data choose template source or expression content, and keep rendered templates on escaping-by-default, server-controlled paths.
recommended_actions: Pass user input only as plain data into templates rather than evaluating it as template syntax.
Review dynamic rendering, previews, and notification templates for attacker-controlled fragments.

framework_variants.summary: Keep Django template structure and filter usage server-controlled, and avoid rendering attacker-supplied template strings or unsafe template engines.
recommended_actions: Remove dynamic template compilation fed by user input.
Review preview tools, CMS-style components, and email rendering for unsafe template evaluation.

framework_variants.summary: Avoid feeding untrusted input into template names, expression language fragments, or dynamic rendering features, and keep template selection constrained.
recommended_actions: Use server-controlled template routing and safe model binding for user data.
Review server-side rendering, email generation, and preview endpoints for template-code injection.

framework_variants.summary: Keep view selection and template structure under server control, and do not compile or evaluate attacker-controlled template content or expressions.
recommended_actions: Pass user values as data only and avoid dynamic template execution paths.
Review Razor generation, email templates, and admin preview features for template-source injection.

framework_variants.summary: Treat template injection as a server-rendering and dynamic content issue: keep JSX, template structure, and render logic server- or build-controlled rather than user-supplied.
recommended_actions: Do not evaluate user input as component code, template fragments, or rich expression syntax.
Review server actions, CMS rendering, and preview tooling for unsafe dynamic evaluation.

validation_steps: Replay template-injection payloads and confirm user input is rendered as inert data rather than executed template logic.
Add tests proving template names, expressions, and source remain server-controlled for the affected path.
Review preview, notification, and CMS-style rendering features for similar dynamic evaluation patterns.
unsafe_alternatives: Filtering a few template metacharacters while still evaluating user-influenced template fragments.
Switching template engines without removing the underlying dynamic evaluation behavior.
Relying on client-side restrictions while server-side rendering paths still accept unsafe template input.

references.label: OWASP Server Side Template Injection Prevention Guidance
CWE-1336 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine

sources.label: OWASP Server Side Template Injection Prevention Guidance
CWE-1336 Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine
